title: Binary Search Tree
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program implementing a binary search tree using objects. Implement:

1. `make_node(value)` - creates a node object {value, left: null, right: null}
2. `insert(root, value)` - inserts a value, returns the (possibly new) root
3. `search(root, value)` - returns true if value exists, false otherwise
4. `inorder(root)` - returns a sorted array of all values via in-order traversal
5. `tree_min(root)` and `tree_max(root)` - return min and max values

Test:
- Insert values in this order: 50, 30, 70, 20, 40, 60, 80
- Print inorder traversal joined with spaces: "20 30 40 50 60 70 80"
- Print search(root, 40): "found 40: true"
- Print search(root, 45): "found 45: false"
- Print min and max: "min: 20", "max: 80"
expected_output:
20 30 40 50 60 70 80
found 40: true
found 45: false
min: 20
max: 80
